steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage opens by saying folk art takes ‘a wide variety of forms,’ then gives storytelling and quilting as examples. The blank sits inside the structure ‘arts as ______ one another as storytelling and quilting.’ The phrase must express that these two forms stand in strong contrast, proving the stated variety.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'useful to': ‘arts as useful to one another as’ — implies they benefit each other, not that they differ. Illogical here. ✗
  • Choice B — 'humble about': ‘arts as humble about one another as’ — makes no grammatical or logical sense. ✗
  • Choice C — 'different from': ‘arts as different from one another as storytelling and quilting’ — completes the comparative structure and directly supports the claim of wide variety. ✓
  • Choice D — 'confused with': ‘arts as confused with one another as’ — implies the forms are mistaken for each other, contradicting the point about variety. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
